title: gh#85: auto-destruct failure notification payload doesn't match `autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema`
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/85.
Backlog section: Bugs.

## Context

Surfaced in [Codex review on #84](https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/pull/84#discussion_r0). The `failed` listener in `packages/worker/src/auto-destruct-worker.ts:94-108` enqueues a notification job with this payload:

```ts
{
  kind: 'auto_destruct_failed',
  postId: job.data.postId,
  platformPostId: job.data.platformPostId,
  correlationId: job.data.correlationId,
  reason: err.message,
  at: new Date().toISOString(),
}
```

But `autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema` at `packages/shared/src/schemas/notifications.ts:77-83` is `.strict()` and requires:

```ts
{
  postId: uuid,
  profileId: uuid,
  errorMessage: string max 2000,
  correlationId: correlationIdSchema,
  occurredAt: datetime ISO string,
}
```

Mismatches (the schema is the contract):

| Sent | Schema expects | Effect |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `kind` | — | strict mode rejects unknown key |
| `platformPostId` | — | strict mode rejects unknown key |
| `reason` | `errorMessage` | missing required field |
| `at` | `occurredAt` | missing required field |
| — | `profileId` | missing required field |

`parseNotificationPayload` in the notification handler will throw `Invalid auto_destruct_failed payload` (see `notifications/handlers/auto-destruct-failed.handler.ts:15-19` calling `autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema.safeParse`), so the user-facing email + in-app bell never fire. This is independent of #15 — #15 changed *when* the listener fires (faster on 401/403), not the payload shape. The payload has been wrong since auto-destruct shipped.

## Why this hasn't been noticed

Probably (a) auto-destruct delete failures are rare on a personal self-hosted instance, (b) the strict-parse failure surfaces only inside the notification worker's own logs, not as a user-visible error. Need to confirm by checking notification-worker logs or a one-off integration test.

## Fix sketch

Two ways to land it; trade-offs in the PR discussion:

**(a) Fix the payload to match the schema (smaller surface area).** Look up `profileId` from the post row inside the failed listener before enqueue (similar to how publish-worker's failed listener already does for its own payload at `publish-worker.ts:347-359`), then enqueue `{ postId, profileId, errorMessage: err.message, correlationId, occurredAt }`. Drop `kind` (already implicit in the job name) and `platformPostId` (not in schema; if useful, propose adding it).

**(b) Widen the schema to accept the existing payload.** Add `platformPostId` as optional, rename `reason` → `errorMessage` at the enqueue site only. This still requires the rename + adding `profileId` lookup, so it isn't strictly less work than (a).

(a) is the consistent pattern — `publish-failed` already resolves `profileId` in its listener.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema.safeParse(payload)` returns `success: true` for the listener's enqueue payload
- [ ] Test: simulate auto-destruct failure → assert notification worker successfully processes the job (no schema-parse error in logs)
- [ ] Pre-existing handler test in `notifications/handlers/__tests__/auto-destruct-failed.test.ts` still passes
- [ ] No change to the listener's *firing conditions* (those were fixed in #15 / PR #84)

## Out of scope

- Schema redesign for unrelated notification kinds
- Adding `platformPostId` to the notification UI/email (separate enhancement)

## Notes

Surfaced by Codex in PR #84 review. Flagging rather than fixing inline because #84's scope is the `UnrecoverableError` retry behavior — this payload bug is independent and predates #15.

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists in current source. packages/worker/src/auto-destruct-worker.ts:93-109 still enqueues JOB_NAMES.autoDestructFailedNotification with kind, postId, platformPostId, correlationId, reason, and at, with no profileId/errorMessage/occurredAt. packages/shared/src/schemas/notifications.ts:77-83 still defines a strict autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema requiring postId, profileId, errorMessage, correlationId, and occurredAt. The handler at packages/worker/src/notifications/handlers/auto-destruct-failed.handler.ts:15-18 still validates via that schema. An executable schema check against current built shared code returned success=false and reported missing profileId/errorMessage/occurredAt plus unrecognized kind/platformPostId/reason/at. The focused Vitest handler test could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to create packages/worker/node_modules/.vite-temp, but the current code and schema validation are sufficient to show the issue remains open.
commands: node --input-type=module -e "import { autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema, JOB_NAMES, assertNotificationPayload } from './packages/shared/dist/index.js'; const payload={kind:'auto_destruct_failed',postId:'11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111',platformPostId:'tweet-123',correlationId:'33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333',reason:'Delete failed',at:'2026-04-28T12:00:00.000Z'}; const result=autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema.safeParse(payload); console.log(JSON.stringify({schemaSafeParseSuccess:result.success, jobAssertSuccess:assertNotificationPayload(JOB_NAMES.autoDestructFailedNotification,payload).success, issues: result.success ? [] : result.error.issues.map(i=>({code:i.code,path:i.path,message:i.message}))}, null, 2));"

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist, but the worker evidence has changed and now matches the schema. packages/worker/src/auto-destruct-worker.ts:95-133 now resolves the post's profileId, skips enqueue if it cannot resolve it, and enqueues JOB_NAMES.autoDestructFailedNotification with postId, profileId, errorMessage, correlationId, and occurredAt only. packages/shared/src/schemas/notifications.ts:77-83 still defines the strict schema requiring exactly those fields. The handler still validates through autoDestructFailedNotificationSchema at packages/worker/src/notifications/handlers/auto-destruct-failed.handler.ts:15-18. A regression test now exists in packages/worker/src/__tests__/auto-destruct-worker.test.ts:367-420 asserting the enqueued payload safe-parses and omits the old kind/platformPostId/reason/at fields. A direct schema check against the current built shared package returned schemaSafeParseSuccess=true and jobAssertSuccess=true for the fixed payload shape. The focused Vitest command could not complete because the read-only sandbox prevented Vite from writing packages/worker/node_modules/.vite-temp, but current source plus the executable schema check show the original payload mismatch is fixed.
commands: rg -n "kind: 'auto_destruct_failed'|platformPostId: job.data.platformPostId|reason: err.message|at: new Date|errorMessage: err.message|occurredAt: new Date|profileId: postRow.profileId" packages/worker/src packages/shared/src -S

[] : result.error.issues.map(i=>({code:i.code,path:i.path,message:i.message}))}, null, 2));"
